title: brachyglottis bidwillii var. viridis
intro: Brachyglottis bidwillii var. viridis, also known as Bidwill's bush or New Zealand lemonwood, is a shrub or small tree native to New Zealand, characterized by its green leaves with a strong citrus-like scent and its yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Brachyglottis bidwillii var. viridis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and provide relief from colds and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
